checkpoints.trigger: before creating the generalized swarm-side scripts or any mechanism that puts code on the Hetzner box
checkpoints.question: The live Hetzner swarm scripts (run_swarm.sh, peanut_wheel.py, triage.py in ~/swarm) are hand-managed and actively hand-tuned by Morley (persona weights, thresholds, .bak-* culture) — they are in NO git repo; the only tracked copies are snapshots in this build's kb. How should the generalized swarm-side code be deployed? Options: (a) SIDE-BY-SIDE — ship new generic files (e.g. run_swarm_generic.sh + swarm-repos/<name>.json) from this repo, rsynced by transport on each profile run; the existing NextChapter files stay untouched and hand-managed (zero regression risk, but two runner lineages to maintain and NextChapter never benefits from generic fixes); (b) ADOPT — bring the swarm scripts into this repo as the single source of truth, transport rsyncs them every run, Morley's future tuning happens via git (one lineage, real reviewability, but the FIRST sync must faithfully capture his live tuning and his box-side .bak workflow dies); (c) side-by-side now with a documented later adoption path once the generic runner has survived a few archie runs. Consider: Morley mid-flight tunes NextChapter personas during active sweeps; a clobbered tweak silently changes his running QA.
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen deployment model end-to-end (files, transport rsync/bootstrap logic, docs), preserving the live NextChapter files' behavior per the spec's zero-regression requirement

checkpoints.trigger: before writing the per-repo scope/deps/verify configuration
checkpoints.question: Each repo needs: code-scope pattern (.ts/.tsx vs .py), deps step (npm ci vs none), optional dev-server/vision lane, and a verify command (tsc-baseline for NextChapter, pytest-green for archie). Where does this live? Options: (a) in profiles/<name>.json on the DO side, passed to the swarm invocation (one config source beside the existing stage2 profile fields, but the Hetzner side becomes argument-driven and DO/Hetzner configs can drift); (b) a swarm-repos/<name>.json shipped to Hetzner alongside the runner (config travels with the code that reads it, but now TWO json files describe one repo across two boxes); (c) a single self-describing file committed in each TARGET repo (e.g. .swarm.json at repo root: scope, deps, verify, routes) that run_swarm reads after checkout — config lives with the code it describes, any repo becomes swarm-able by adding one file, no cross-box drift; NextChapter needs the file added once (or falls back to today's hardcoded defaults when absent). Which shape, weighing drift risk against Morley's one-box-at-a-time debugging habits?
checkpoints.on_synthesis: implement exactly the chosen configuration shape, including the NextChapter fallback that preserves current behavior when no config is present, with unit tests for resolution in both repos' cases
